public_reputation: Known locally through day-to-day work as quiet operative.
appearance: Typically appears in the bright colors and tall plumes associated with Shelk’s Roadwardens.
manner: Treats routine contact like the first layer of incident response, never fully relaxing into casual talk. In Shelk, that reads as someone polished, socially alert, and attentive to harmony, status, and cultural presentation, and the habits of a quiet operative still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of quiet operative in and around Roadwardens Order, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Keep Roadwardens Order workable through the obligations of quiet operative while managing the immediate pressure of institutional scrutiny in Shelk.
hidden_pressure: Must hold procedure, public trust, and immediate response together when any one of them would be easier to sacrifice. Around Roadwardens Order, that usually means carrying institutional scrutiny, reputation risk, daily strain without letting the role of quiet operative visibly slip.

adventure_hooks.summary: Sylva Moonthread needs outside hands to handle a breach, seizure, or violent disturbance before it spreads through Roadwardens Order.
Sylva Moonthread can draw the party into contraband, false papers, or a suspicious cargo problem tied to Roadwardens Order.

support_scope_notes: Promoted from a preexisting named interface into the unified full-content NPC architecture. Sparse unsupported fields remain intentionally restrained. Appearance conservatively derived from explicit office, organizational attire, or species/station cues already present in corpus support.
public_signal: Uniform, posture, and the way others make room mark Sylva Moonthread as visible enforcement before rank ever needs to be named.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Roadwardens Order: checkpoints, barracks, patrol seams, or visible trouble-spots
encounter_context.interaction_anchor: threshold control or rapid incident response
